items.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
items.summary: Google added a new Chrome workflow that turns saved prompts into reusable one-click tools so users can trigger repeatable AI actions without rebuilding prompts each time.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: browser-native AI is shifting from one-off prompting toward lightweight workflow automation that users can keep close to where work already happens.

items.title: Prepay for the Gemini API to get more control over your spend
items.summary: Google introduced prepaid Gemini API credits so developers can manage budgets more tightly and get clearer control over AI infrastructure spend before usage accrues.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: as model costs become a line-item decision, pricing controls and billing flexibility are becoming product features in their own right.

items.title: Introducing Learn Mode: your personal coding tutor in Google Colab
items.summary: Google launched Learn Mode in Colab to guide users through code and notebook workflows with more tutor-like step-by-step support instead of only returning direct answers.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI coding products are increasingly differentiating on teaching and guidance, not just raw task completion.

items.title: Generate 3D models and interactive charts with the Gemini app
items.summary: Google expanded the Gemini app so users can create 3D models and interactive charts directly from prompts, pushing the product further into visual and data-rich creation tasks.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: consumer AI assistants are steadily broadening from text chat into richer visual outputs that can slot into real workflows faster.

items.title: Copilot SDK enters public preview for app and workflow builders
items.summary: GitHub opened the Copilot SDK in public preview so teams can embed Copilot's agentic capabilities more directly into their own apps, workflows, and platform services.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agent platforms are moving from standalone products into infrastructure that other software teams can wire into their own surfaces.

items.title: GitHub makes organization-wide Copilot custom instructions generally available
items.summary: GitHub made organization custom instructions generally available for Copilot Business and Enterprise so admins can steer default assistant behavior across teams.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise AI rollouts increasingly depend on shared defaults and governance controls, not just individual user prompts.

items.title: GitHub broadens Copilot cloud agent from PR tasks to research and planning work
items.summary: GitHub expanded Copilot cloud agent beyond pull-request workflows so it can take on more research, planning, and coding work before changes ever reach review.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: coding assistants are shifting from inline helpers to longer-horizon agents that can own more of the software loop.

items.title: GitHub adds cloud-agent activity into Copilot usage metrics
items.summary: GitHub updated Copilot usage metrics so cloud-agent active user counts roll into reporting, giving teams a clearer read on how agent workflows are actually being adopted.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: once agent deployments grow, measurement and accountability become as important as model capability.

items.title: Anthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network
items.summary: Anthropic launched the Claude Partner Network with a $100 million commitment to help partner organizations support enterprise adoption of Claude.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier model vendors are scaling through service ecosystems and implementation partners, not just direct product sales.

items.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-generation compute
items.summary: Anthropic said it is expanding work with Google and Broadcom to secure multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute for future model development and deployment.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: compute access remains one of the clearest strategic bottlenecks in the frontier model race.

items.title: Introducing The Anthropic Institute
items.summary: Anthropic launched The Anthropic Institute as a new effort focused on preparing for the broader societal challenges created by more powerful AI systems.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: labs are increasingly building public-policy and governance arms alongside product and research teams.

items.title: AWS and OpenAI announce multi-year strategic partnership
items.summary: AWS and OpenAI announced a multi-year partnership that will put advanced OpenAI workloads on AWS infrastructure as the companies deepen their cloud relationship.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: infrastructure alliances are becoming a strategic lever for model labs trying to secure compute and distribution at scale.

items.title: ModMed selects AWS as the cloud provider for the AI-powered practice
items.summary: Amazon said ModMed is standardizing on AWS to support an AI-powered practice stack built for specialty medicine workflows and clinical software operations.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: healthcare AI adoption is increasingly tied to domain-specific software stacks rather than general-purpose model demos.

items.title: Q&A: MIT SHASS and the future of education in the age of AI
items.summary: MIT's School of Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences argued that human understanding and broad education matter even more as AI reshapes how students learn and work.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the education debate around AI is moving from novelty tools to the long-term shape of how people think, learn, and reason.

items.title: New technique makes AI models leaner and faster while they are still learning
items.summary: MIT researchers introduced CompreSSM, a method that trims model complexity during training so AI systems can keep learning while becoming leaner and more efficient.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: efficiency work is becoming a core frontier in AI because it cuts compute cost without waiting for the next giant model release.
